input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property, which `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets to `true` automatically. Place a conditional `PropertyGroup` in your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Properties shared by all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP migration: only applies to test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** The `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package sets `IsTestProject=true` in its imported `.props`/`.targets`. Since your test projects reference that package and your library projects don't, the condition activates only for tests — no naming-convention hacks needed.

**Caveat:** `IsTestProject` is set by the SDK package's imports, so it may not be available until after those imports run. If the condition doesn't fire, move the block to a `Directory.Build.targets` file instead (same syntax), which is evaluated later in the build order.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally apply MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that certain properties (like `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties) only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or uses a naming convention.

The cleanest approach for MTP migration is to condition on the `IsTestProject` property, which is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. You can also use a naming convention like `$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))`.


Now I'll provide a practical answer with the specific conditional syntax.
